caption:
Aaron Judge is on pace for 61 home runs this season.

12 home runs through 32 games. Barry Bonds set the all-time single-season record with 73 home runs in 2001. No one has hit 62 or more since. Reaching that territory would be one of the most remarkable power seasons in baseball history.

Is this the real deal? 👇

#MLB #Baseball #AaronJudge #FullCountID

story.record_context: Barry Bonds set the all-time single-season record with 73 home runs in 2001. No one has hit 62 or more since. Reaching that territory would be one of the most remarkable power seasons in baseball history.
story.label: Aaron Judge is on pace for 61 home runs this season
story.lede: Aaron Judge has 12 home runs through 32 games this season. At that rate, he projects for 61 over a full 162-game season. The all-time single-season record is 73, set by Barry Bonds in 2001.
story.context: Barry Bonds set the all-time single-season record with 73 home runs in 2001. No one has hit 62 or more since. Reaching that territory would be one of the most remarkable power seasons in baseball history.
